title: moraea simulans
intro: Moraea simulans, also known as the 'Yellow Corn Lily' or 'Yellow Corn Marigold', is a plant species in the family Iridaceae, native to the southern parts of Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its roots, which contain anthraquinones and anthocyanins with potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Moraea simulans include its ability to treat digestive issues, such as constipation, diarrhea, and stomach ulcers, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Moraea simulans can be prepared as an herbal infusion by steeping the dried roots in hot water or as a tincture by extracting the roots in a solvent like ethanol or glycerin.
